mnemonics.title: QUEUE OPERATIONS
mnemonics.content: ENQUEUE ADDS TO REAR, DEQUEUE REMOVES FROM FRONT - EARR (Enqueue At Rear, Remove at Rear confuses - remember FIFO!)

acronyms.usage: The fundamental principle of queue data structure describing the order of element insertion and removal
Operation to insert an element at the rear of the queue

acronyms.expansion: Delete from Queue / Remove from Queue
acronyms.usage: Operation to remove an element from the front of the queue
Queue that allows insertion and deletion from both ends
The end where elements are added (enqueued)
The end from where elements are removed (dequeued)

visualTips.concept: Queue as a line of people
visualTips.visualization: Imagine people standing in a ticket counter line. The person at the front gets the ticket first and leaves. New people join at the back of the line.

visualTips.visualize: Think of a train with an engine (FRONT) and a caboose (REAR). Passengers board at the caboose (enqueue) and exit from the engine (dequeue).
A circular wheel where the rear position wraps around to the front when it reaches the end, like a rotating serving platter at a dinner table.
A hallway with lockers numbered 1 to 10. If people keep entering from one end but no one leaves from the other, locker 10 gets filled and no one else can enter even if lockers 1-5 are empty.

analogies.analogy: A restaurant queue outside a popular food stall. People join at the end of the line and the first person in line gets served first and leaves.
Emergency room in a hospital where a critically ill patient is treated before others who arrived earlier but have less severe conditions.
A two-way street where cars can enter and exit from both ends of the road.
A chain of people passing buckets of water. Each person (node) knows only the person behind them. The first person passes to the second, and so on.
